Motion To Quash The Subpoena Of Marjorie Cozzie And For Sanctions Against Jasmine Reedfoley Lardner Llp And Defendant Luke Thomas Smith
Off Calendar Dec-28-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JULIAN GONZALEZ's Motion To Quash The Subpoena Of Marjorie Cozzie And For Sanctions Against Jasmine Reed, Foley Lardner LLP, And Defendant Luke Thomas Smith.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion granted in part; denied in part. The Court agrees with Defendant that a deposition of Ms. Cozzie should be allowed, but also agrees with Plaintiff that Judge Ulmer's order of December 13, 2021 limits the scope of the deposition to Plaintiff's individualized treatment no more than 24 hours before the accident. However, Defendant is allowed to pursue other lines of inquiry including generalized treatment protocols and facility operations beyond the 24 hour period. None of the records that Judge Ulmer ordered be returned or destroyed shall be used in the deposition. Ms. Cozzie's deposition shall occur by January 7, 2022 unless the motion to continue the trial date is granted before then. Sanctions denied.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JULIAN GONZALEZ's Motion To Quash Va Subpoena And Request For Sanctions Against Jasmine Reed, Foley & Lardner, LLP, And Defendant Luke Thomas Smith:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Plaintiff's motion to quash is denied. Consistent with prior discovery orders, the scope of the amended subpoena is sufficiently tailored to records relating to the alleged lost earnings, physical injuries and associated pain and suffering claimed by the plaintiff at his deposition and as set forth in the amended statement of damages. Plaintiff's counsel shall have a "first look: at the records, utilizing the protocol set forth in the discovery order dated 10/16/2021, except that, in the event that a good faith dispute over withholding cannot be resolved, either party should file a new motion. Defendant's counsel shall be responsible for ensuring that the witness is aware of this order, including the "first look" protocol. Sanctions denied.

Order is signed.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoena With Leave Of Court And Request To Seal Prior Filings Which Include Privileged Records
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. KIMBERLY E. LEVY, Esq. of Dolan Law Firm (Tel: 415-421-2800) for plaintiff Julian Gonzalez;
Atty. JASMINE REED, Esq. of Foley & Lardner LLP (Tel: 312-832-4500) for defendant Luke Thomas Smith.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF JULIAN GONZALEZ's Motion To Quash Subpoena With Leave Of Court And Request To Seal Prior Filings Which Include Privileged Records.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The court adopts the Judge Pro Tem's report and recommendation in part.

The motion to quash is granted.

Defendant Smith shall destroy all of the documents/records that he obtained due to the erroneous transfer from Fort Help Mission Inc. Smith is prohibited from reviewing, using, or disclosing these documents/records.

Smith shall withdraw the court filings that cite these records and re-file those filings with appropriate redactions.

The Fort Help records that pertain to treatment of Plaintiff's left leg or medications/drugs taken within 24 hours before the collision shall be produced.

Plaintiff should have a first look at the Fort Help records with the following
protocol being used:

1. Ace Attorney Service, the service used by defendant to administer the subpoenas, will copy and add Bates numbers to all pages obtained from Fort Help as soon as possible, and provide them electronically or by mail to plaintiff's counsel only;

2. Within seven calendar days from when plaintiff's counsel receives the documents, plaintiff's counsel shall create and electronically serve upon defendant's counsel a detailed privilege log of:

A. Each page Plaintiff is seeking to withhold on account of a recognized privilege (including without limitation privacy). Plaintiff's counsel may not withhold entire pages unless the entire page is privileged; and

B. All redactions made by Plaintiff's counsel on account of a recognized privilege; the privilege log must be sufficiently detailed, so as to allow defense counsel to determine the validity of the asserted reason for the redaction or withholding. At the least, the privilege log must identify author(s), date(s), a description of the withheld document(s) or redaction(s), and the legal basis for the withholding/redaction.

3. Also within seven calendar days from when plaintiff's counsel received the documents, plaintiff's counsel shall produce electronically or by mail to defendant all documents that plaintiff has not withheld.

No sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Sep-09-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly Levy, Esq. (415-421-2800; kimberly.levy@dolanlawfirm.com) of Dolan Law Firm for Plaintiff.
Jasmine Reed, Esq. (312-832-4500; jreed@foley.com) of Foley & Lardner LLP for Defendant via ZOOM videoconference.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT LUKE SMITH'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Against Plaintiff Julian Gonzalez:

Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant's motion for summary adjudication on the negligence per se cause of action, the negligence cause of action, and the affirmative defense of assumption of risk, is denied. Defendant fails to shift the burden as to any of plaintiff's causes of action. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections to the traffic collision report and insurance adjuster's determination of fault are sustained. The Court declines to consider the additional evidence improperly submitted with Defendant's reply. (See Jay v. Mahaffey (2013) 218 Cal.App.4th 1522, 1537 ["The general rule of motion practice, which applies here, is that new evidence is not permitted with reply papers. This principle is most prominent in the context of summary judgment motions . . . ."].)

[see word minutes for details.]

Order is signed.
